sources.query: spawnClaudeCodeProcess custom spawn option, canUseTool signature, query and listSessions APIs, stream-json, permission modes, hooks config
findings: canUseTool TypeScript signature: (toolName: string, input: Record<string, unknown>, options: { signal: AbortSignal; suggestions?: PermissionUpdate[]; blockedPath?: string; decisionReason?: string; toolUseID: string; agentID?: string }) => Promise<PermissionResult>
PermissionResult shape: { behavior: 'allow' | 'deny'; updatedInput?: Record; updatedPermissions?: PermissionUpdate[]; message?: string; interrupt?: boolean }
Permission modes: default | dontAsk | acceptEdits | bypassPermissions | plan | auto
Dynamic mid-session mode change via q.setPermissionMode(mode) on query object — important for CAP-025 (SDK bridge can escalate to dontAsk on long-poll timeout or inversely tighten on high-risk)
askUserQuestion tool has previewFormat ToolConfig: 'markdown' | 'html' — CAP-030 form renderer should honor this
options.suggestions carries PermissionUpdate[] — CAP-028 (Approve-and-Remember) can read these to pre-fill the rule creation UI instead of inferring from scratch
options.blockedPath + decisionReason give context for why a permission request fired — useful metadata to persist in approval_requests for debugging
options.toolUseID is REQUIRED and stable — use as idempotency key for approval_requests to avoid duplicates on hook retries

GAP: Context7 does not surface a spawnClaudeCodeProcess custom-spawn option in the public SDK docs. The workforce research doc asserts this exists but it may be preview/internal. Recommend treating CAP-076 as an abstraction layer the AGENT implements (via child_process.spawn over docker exec / ssh / wsl), NOT as an SDK option. The SDK query() runs in-process; the abstraction lives outside the SDK.

sources.query: createContainer HostConfig security options, attach, demuxStream, wait, stats streaming
findings: container.attach({stream: true, stdout: true, stderr: true}) returns a multiplexed stream when Tty: false
container.modem.demuxStream(stream, stdoutWritable, stderrWritable) splits the multiplexed stream — confirmed exactly as CAP-086 specifies
Tty MUST be false for demuxing — TTY mode returns a single combined stream (matches research doc advice against -t flag)
container.wait(callback) returns data.StatusCode on exit — use for CAP-085 exit code capture

container.stats({stream: true}) emits JSON chunks with cpu_stats.cpu_usage.total_usage, memory_stats.usage, networks.eth0.rx_bytes — CAP-087 needs to derive cpuPercent from cpu_stats via (current - previous) / (system_current - system_previous) * online_cpus * 100
container.stats({stream: false}) returns a single snapshot — use for on-demand stats queries
container.logs({stdout, stderr, follow, timestamps, tail}) supports historical + real-time follow with the same demuxStream helper — alternative to attach for late-joiners

docker.run(image, cmd, outputStream, options, callback) is a single-call helper that creates, starts, attaches, and waits — good fit for CAP-089 (async setup commands in temp container) to avoid manual lifecycle dance

gaps_and_risks.risk: spawnClaudeCodeProcess option mentioned in workforce research may not exist in current SDK. Plan to implement spawn abstraction at the AGENT level (child_process over docker exec / ssh / wsl) rather than as an SDK option. If the option turns out to exist, CAP-076 can be refactored to pass it through.
Docker stats JSON returns raw numerators and denominators, not cpuPercent directly. Stats reporter must compute delta over a sampling interval. Document the formula in the spec.

Litestream Docker sidecar config not yet verified. Research doc references it but specific compose config not pulled. Plan to use Litestream's official docker image and mount the same sqlite-data volume; defer detailed config to implementation sprint.

Squid vs tinyproxy choice unresolved in research. Recommendation: start with tinyproxy for minimal surface area, allowlist api.anthropic.com + registry.npmjs.org + github IP ranges via filter config. Squid only if we need caching/logging later.

notes: Research enrichment intentionally limited. The 7 source research documents are comprehensive for most capabilities. Targeted Context7 lookups focused on the two libraries with the highest implementation-detail risk: the Claude Agent SDK (canUseTool / permission modes — cross-cuts the approval workflow) and dockerode (cross-cuts the container orchestration domain). Other capabilities either derive cleanly from the source docs or have implementation details that can be resolved during sprint planning.
